channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ICC Rejects Duterte Lawyers' Appeal on Confirmation of Charges for Drug War Killings
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) Pre-Trial Chamber I has rejected the appeal request of former President Rodrigo Duterte's legal team regarding the confirmation of charges linked to killings dur...

item.title: Fedora and SUSE Remove Deepin Desktop Due to Security Concerns
item.description: Deepin Desktop Environment (DDE), once praised for its aesthetics and potential to revolutionize Linux desktops, has faced persistent security concerns that have led major distributions to drop it. In...

item.title: Njombe Region to Develop Major Iron Ore and Mining Projects for Economic Growth
item.description: Njombe Region dey prepare for big economic changes as major iron ore mining projects dey move forward, mainly for Ludewa District. Di National Development Corporation (NDC) dey work with foreign inves...

item.title: Philippines News Update: Bato Dela Rosa Arrest, Sara Duterte Impeachment, and Ghost Project Investigation

item.title: Shared Ownership as the Key to Scaling African Businesses
item.description: Africa is at a critical economic junction, with a lot of talent, innovation, and market opportunities sprouting across the continent. Yet, many African enterprises struggle to grow from small ventures...

item.title: Hamzat Praises Smooth APC Primary, Urges Peace Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Deputy Governor of Lagos State, Kadri Hamzat, don commend how the All Progressives Congress (APC) conduct their governorship primary across Lagos without wahala. Hamzat, wey also be the consensus ...

item.title: Former Special Task Force Member Accused of Murdering Witness D in Brakpan
item.description: The case against former SAPS special task force member Matipandile Sotheni, accused of murdering Witness D, centres on WhatsApp messages, phone records, and vehicle tracking data. Investigating office...

item.title: Paul McCartney required to adjust garden plans following council and neighbour feedback
item.description: Paul McCartney has been asked to modify his garden plans at his St John’s Wood residence in London following concerns raised by a neighbour and the local council. The musician initially applied to rem...

item.title: DR Congo cancels World Cup farewell events amid Ebola outbreak
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has cancelled its three-day World Cup preparation training camp and a farewell event for fans in Kinshasa due to an ongoing Ebola outbreak in the eastern regions...

item.title: Analysis of the Proposed African Charter on Family, Sovereignty and Values
item.description: Between 2023 and 2025, plenty conferences happen for Entebbe, Uganda wey lead to the drafting of one African Charter on Family, Sovereignty and Values. This conference dey backed by Inter-parliamentar...

item.title: Ricky Stuart questions consistency of NRL refereeing after Raiders loss
item.description: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart expressed frustration with NRL refereeing following his team's defeat to the Redcliffe Dolphins. Stuart specifically criticised referee Todd Smart, accusing officia...

item.title: Addressing Ebola Outbreaks in Conflict Zones Requires Tackling Violence and Weak Health Systems
item.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is facing another Ebola outbreak, this time identified as the Bundibugyo ebolavirus. Unlike the Zaire strain, Bundibugyo currently has no approved vaccines o...

item.title: Stanbic Bank expands RISE Mama Lishe programme to Dodoma, trains 320 food vendors with business skills and energy-efficient stoves
item.description: Stanbic Bank Tanzania has expanded its RISE Mama Lishe programme to Dodoma region, where it is empowering 320 food entrepreneurs, mostly women and youth known as Mama Lishe and Baba Lishe. These small...

item.title: Acting AG Blanche signs IRS settlement granting Trump family immunity, raising legal and ethical questions
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche has signed documents that grant President Donald Trump and his family immunity from IRS scrutiny, sparking controversy over the legality and ethics of the set...

item.title: Gordon Ramsay and Tom Hiddleston Withdraw from Celebrity Traitors Due to Scheduling Conflicts

item.title: Gen Z-led ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ gains massive social media following in India
item.description: A new youth-oriented political group in India, the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), has gone viral, amassing nearly 15 million Instagram followers in under a week, surpassing the ruling Bharatiya Janata P...

item.title: A Retrospective on Stephen Colbert’s Most Memorable Late Show Moments

item.title: Bob Uecker and Bob Gibson's 1964 Team Photo Prank and Its Consequences
item.description: In 1964, during the St. Louis Cardinals team photo session, backup catcher Bob Uecker convinced ace pitcher Bob Gibson to hold hands as a lighthearted prank. Both players smiled for the photograph, ai...

item.title: Government Ramps Up Preparations for 2026 Local Government Elections
item.description: The South African government has intensified efforts to ensure that the 2026 Local Government Elections, scheduled for 4 November, are conducted efficiently and transparently. Cooperative Governance a...
